prompt:
generate an image of the following:The top 1% of the world are the filthy rich billionaires who bask in their dirty money they acquired through the exploitation of the people who work for the system they created.
A dining table where 99 people share one small plate and one person sits surrounded by mountains of food. Wide-angle editorial illustration, cinematic lighting.
A long wooden banquet table stretches across the image.
On the far left, ninety-nine people crowd together sharing.
one small plate of food, their faces drawn and tired.
On the far right, one well-dressed person sits alone.
surrounded by an overflowing feast that covers half the table.
The left side of the image is in shadow, the right side
bathed in warm golden light.
Mood: moral weight, imbalance made visceral.
Color palette: warm amber on the right, cold desaturated blue-grey on the left.
No text, no labels, no numbers visible. dont give it the cartoon style. make it look real and life like. 
make the mood suffocating, like u can feel the hunger of the poor people both literally and metaphorically. and there should be an evil hunger in the rich person's face too, hunger for more and more such that it would never suffice him. 
give it satellite image quality and aesthetic, make it look real and documentary photo style. like it is a scientific diagram rendered as an oil painting. 
create the rich man's wife standing next to him laden with jewels and diamonds, laughing and pointing at the poor. make it painterly, abstract, dreamlike. 
warm ochre and burnt sienna on the right and large areas of negative space in deep prussian blue shadows hanging heavy over the crowd on the left. generate it in editorial oil painting style.
 make the jewels on the woman brighter, make the rich man look fat and older. make the poor people's expression sad and scared and hungry, some of them looking at the food and some looking at the rich. 
No text, no labels, no numbers visible. no border
insight:
The top 1% of the world are the filthy rich billionaires who bask in their dirty money they acquired through the exploitation of the people who work for the system they created;
   this world is just a money-making machine for them and no amount of money will ever be able to satiate their hunger.
